Need:

3. Optional squash to digest for audit trail

- sling.go sets thing.IsWisp but never uses it
- bd mol run has no --wisp flag

 {"id":"gt-9wv0","title":"gt spawn should verify daemon is running for polecat triggering","description":"gt spawn notifies the Deacon (via mail) that a polecat was started, expecting the Deacon to trigger it once Claude is ready. But if the daemon isn't running, the mail sits unread and the polecat never gets triggered.

## Current Behavior
1. gt spawn starts polecat session
2. gt spawn sends POLECAT_STARTED to deacon/
3. (assumes daemon will trigger polecat)

## Problem
If gt daemon isn't running, step 3 never happens and polecat sits at prompt.

## Solution
In gt spawn, after session start:
1. Check if daemon is running (gt daemon status)
2. If not running, either:
   a. Start daemon: gt daemon start
   b. Or warn user: 'Daemon not running, polecat may not auto-start'

## Alternative
The user can manually trigger with gt nudge, but automated flow should work.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:03.71521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:03.71521-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9wv0","depends_on_id":"gt-bjft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:03:12.187224-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-9za0","title":"Mol Mall: Molecule marketplace distribution","description":"Design the Mol Mall distribution mechanism for sharing molecules.

## Core Insight

Mol Mall is just a registry of molecules.jsonl fragments. No special format needed.

## Distribution Model

```
Mol Mall (registry)
    │
    │ bd mol search "security"
    ▼
Molecule catalog (searchable index)
    │
    │ bd mol install mol-security-scan
    ▼
Download molecules.jsonl fragment
    │
    │ Append to ~/.beads/molecules.jsonl
    ▼
Available locally: bd mol list
```

## Registry Format

Simple JSON index served from HTTPS:

```json
{
  "molecules": [
    {
      "id": "mol-security-scan",
      "title": "Security Scan",
      "description": "OWASP Top 10 vulnerability checker",
      "labels": ["plugin", "code-review", "tier:sonnet"],
      "author": "anthropic",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "url": "https://mol-mall.anthropic.com/molecules/mol-security-scan.jsonl"
    }
  ]
}
```

## CLI Commands

```bash
bd mol search <query>           # Search registry
bd mol info <id>                # Show molecule details
bd mol install <id>             # Download and install
bd mol uninstall <id>           # Remove from catalog
bd mol update                   # Update all installed molecules
```

## Installation Locations

```
~/.beads/molecules.jsonl        # User-level (default install location)
~/gt/.beads/molecules.jsonl     # Town-level (shared across rigs)
.beads/molecules.jsonl          # Project-level (team-specific)
```

## Version Management

Molecules can have versions via labels: `version:1.0.0`
Install specific version: `bd mol install mol-security-scan@1.0.0`

## Auth (Future)

Private registries could require auth token.
Enterprise Mol Malls for internal distribution.

## Related

- gt-u818: Plugin System (plugins ARE molecules)
- molecular-chemistry.md: Format documentation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T05:14:35.698369-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T05:14:35.698369-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-kabx","title":"Build context self-check into patrol molecules","description":"Patrol molecules (witness-patrol, refinery-patrol, deacon-patrol) should have
explicit context self-check as part of each cycle:

After each patrol cycle step:
- Self-assess context pressure
- If high: complete current step, then initiate handoff
- If OK: continue to next cycle

This could be:
1. Explicit step in molecule: 'context-check'
2. Guidance in step instructions: 'Before continuing, assess context...'
3. Protocol baked into the patrol loop description

The key is making it unavoidable - not optional guidance that gets ignored.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:46:57.067465-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:46:57.067465-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-kcee","title":"Witness commands: gt witness start/stop/status needed","description":"## Summary

No `gt witness` command exists. The witness should:
- Monitor polecats for stuck/idle state
- Nudge polecats that seem blocked
- Report status to mayor
- Handle polecat lifecycle

## Expected Commands

```bash
gt witness start gastown   # Start witness for rig
gt witness stop gastown    # Stop witness
gt witness status          # Show witness status
```

## Current State

- Witness directory exists: /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/witness/
- Has state.json but no active process
- gt status shows 'Witnesses: 0'

## Context

Full polecat flow needs witness monitoring for production use.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:55:24.079671-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.856942-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.856942-08:00"}

-{"id":"gt-kjnt","title":"Polecat Mood Plugin (standard)","description":"Standard witness plugin that assesses polecat emotional state.

## Location
`<rig>/plugins/witness/polecat-mood/`

## Mood Emojis
| Mood | Emoji | Detection |
|------|-------|-----------|
| working | 😺 | Active tool calls, making progress |
| productive | 😸 | Completing tasks, tests passing |
| idle | 🐱 | Waiting at prompt, no recent activity |
| confident | 😼 | Self-reviewing, about to submit |
| struggling | 😿 | Repeated errors, test failures |
| stuck | 🙀 | No progress for 10+ min |
| done | 😽 | Work complete, requesting shutdown |
| blocked | 😾 | Explicitly waiting on dependency |

## Plugin Config (plugin.yaml)
```yaml
name: polecat-mood
description: Assess polecat emotional state from session activity
trigger: patrol
tier: haiku
input: session-capture
```

## Output
Plugin outputs gt commands:
```
gt polecat mood Rictus 😺
gt polecat mood Nux 😿
```

## Customization
Users can fork and modify prompt.md to change assessment criteria or add custom moods.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:12.841134-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:12.841134-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kjnt","depends_on_id":"gt-u818","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:20.444775-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-kjnt","title":"Polecat Mood Plugin (standard)","description":"Standard witness plugin molecule that assesses polecat emotional state.

## Molecule Definition

```json
{
  "id": "mol-polecat-mood",
  "title": "Assess mood for {{polecat_name}}",
  "description": "Analyze captured output and determine emotional state.\n\nVars:\n- {{polecat_name}} - The polecat to assess\n- {{captured_output}} - Recent tmux capture\n\nOutput: gt polecat mood {{polecat_name}} <emoji>",
  "labels": ["template", "plugin", "witness", "tier:haiku"],
  "issue_type": "task"
}
```

## Mood Emojis

```
😺 working     Active tool calls, making progress
😸 productive  Completing tasks, tests passing
🐱 idle        Waiting at prompt, no recent activity
😼 confident   Self-reviewing, about to submit
😿 struggling  Repeated errors, test failures
🙀 stuck       No progress for 10+ min
😽 done        Work complete, requesting shutdown
😾 blocked     Explicitly waiting on dependency
```

## Bonding

During witness patrol plugin-run step:

```bash
bd mol bond mol-polecat-mood $PATROL_WISP \
  --ref mood-{{polecat_name}} \
  --var polecat_name=ace \
  --var captured_output="$TMUX_CAPTURE"
```

## Installation

```bash
bd mol install mol-polecat-mood  # From Mol Mall
```

## Customization

Fork the molecule and modify description to change assessment criteria or add custom moods. Install your fork to override.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:12.841134-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T05:07:39.495061-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kjnt","depends_on_id":"gt-u818","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:17:20.444775-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-kmn","title":"Batch Work System: Integration branch, merge queue, landing","description":"## Overview

This epic tracks batch work coordination in GGT. Key insight: **work is a stream, not discrete swarms**.

There are no "swarm IDs" - the epic IS the grouping, the merge queue IS the coordination. "Swarming an epic" is colloquial for spawning multiple workers on its children.

## Architecture

- **Epics** group related work
- **Issues** are individual tasks  
- **Dependencies** encode ordering (multi-wave emerges automatically)
- **Merge queue** coordinates completed work
- **Workers** process issues independently

See Key Decision #11 in docs/architecture.md: "Work is a Stream (No Swarm IDs)"

## What This Epic Covers

1. Integration branch management for batch merges
2. Refinery semantic merge processing
3. Witness landing protocol (cleanup)
4. Report generation (epic-based, not swarm-ID-based)
5. Git safety auditing
6. Work reconciliation

## What Was Removed

- Swarm IDs (sw-1, sw-2, etc.)
- Per-swarm directories (.gastown/swarms/<id>/)
- Swarm manifest/state/events files
- swarm-started/swarm-landed beads

These were redundant - beads already provides hierarchy, status, dependencies, and history.

## References

- Architecture: docs/architecture.md (Key Decision #11, Multi-Wave Work Processing)
- PGT: swarm/manager.py, swarm/landing.py (behavioral reference, but ignore swarm ID patterns)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:08:15.339127-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:33.208338-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-kmn.1","title":"Swarm package: Swarm, SwarmTask, SwarmState types","description":"Define core swarm types.

## Types (in `internal/swarm/`)

```go
// SwarmState represents swarm lifecycle
type SwarmState string
const (
    SwarmCreated   SwarmState = "created"
    SwarmActive    SwarmState = "active"
    SwarmMerging   SwarmState = "merging"
    SwarmLanded    SwarmState = "landed"
    SwarmFailed    SwarmState = "failed"
    SwarmCancelled SwarmState = "cancelled"
)

// Swarm references a beads epic that tracks swarm work
type Swarm struct {
    ID          string      // matches beads epic ID
    RigName     string
    EpicID      string      // beads epic tracking this swarm
    BaseCommit  string      // git SHA all workers branch from
    Integration string      // integration branch name
    State       SwarmState
    CreatedAt   time.Time
    Workers     []string    // polecat names assigned
}

// SwarmTask represents a single task in the swarm (maps to beads issue)
type SwarmTask struct {
    IssueID   string  // beads issue ID
    Assignee  string  // polecat name
    Branch    string  // worker branch name
    State     string  // mirrors beads status
}
```

## Note

Swarm state is primarily stored IN beads. These types are in-memory representations for the SwarmManager to work with. No separate manifest.json files.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:08:30.364047-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T13:31:56.20815-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T13:31:56.20815-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.1","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:08:30.364431-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.1","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:20.646487-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-kmn.10","title":"Batch work landing report generation","description":"Generate reports on batch work completion.

## Trigger

When all children of an epic are closed, generate a completion report.

## Report Contents

- Epic metadata (ID, title, created by, duration)
- Task summary (completed counts, timing)
- Per-task details (issue, assignee, time taken)
- Git stats (commits merged, lines changed)
- Cleanup stats (branches deleted)

## Output Locations

1. Mail to Mayor - summary report
2. Optional: save to file with --save flag

## Command

```bash
gt report --epic <epic-id> [--save report.md]
```

## Format

Markdown with sections:
- Summary
- Tasks
- Workers
- Timeline

## Note

This replaces the swarm-based reporting (gt-662). Reports are generated from epic/issue data, not from separate swarm state files.

## Reference

PGT: swarm/report.py (for format ideas, ignore swarm ID patterns)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:23.242931-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:27.99208-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-kmn.10","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:10:23.243287-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
